paragraphs.structure: Five sentences. Opens with misdirection and correction, expands into territorial catalogue, pivots on counter-expectation, then tracks reputation through combat history to present supremacy.
rhetorical-dna: Triadic enumeration (vineyards, pastures, islands) followed by contrast pivot
"Think not... that" construction inverting expectation against appearance
Formal register with archaic verb forms (affecteth, carrieth, oweth)
Climactic ranking structure: third-best to peer to supreme
Simile for delicacy (silver birch) counterweighted by martial record
paragraphs.respecification-seed: A figure's refined appearance belies deadly combat prowess and noble standing.

paragraphs.structure: Two long sentences. First captures ceremonial transition with sensory fade; second expands into branching catalogue of leisure activities, closing on temporal span of celebration.
rhetorical-dna: Synaesthetic music metaphor (strains sighed and lost themselves)
Anaphoric "some to..." listing creating parallel rhythmic waves
Elevated diction maintaining courtly atmosphere (pleasaunces, disport, caroused)
Periodic sentence delaying main verb while accumulating detail
Closing formula invoking duration and delight
paragraphs.respecification-seed: A formal gathering disperses into varied pleasures across a festive day.

paragraphs.structure: Single sentence. Subject-verb-purpose chain linking movement, concealment, and deliberation in one breath.
rhetorical-dna: Tripartite action sequence (rose, withdrew, to determine)
Spatial transition signaling shift to serious business
Formal phrasing ("determine of their answer") maintaining register
Brief and decisive after preceding expansiveness
paragraphs.respecification-seed: Leaders withdraw privately to deliberate an urgent response.

paragraphs.structure: Six sentences building strategic argument. Opens with enemy's cunning timing, catalogues losses, pivots to bitter irony of betrayal after shared victory, projects enemy's calculation and readiness.
rhetorical-dna: Bitter parallelism contrasting sacrifice against opportunism
Precise numerical detail (thirty-three sunken, fourteen remain) grounding stakes
Rhetorical questions sharpening accusation of treachery
"mouth-friends" and "treason" vocabulary marking moral outrage
Conditional projection ("if he get the answer he knoweth") showing enemy's certainty
paragraphs.respecification-seed: A leader analyzes how an ally's betrayal exploits their weakened state after costly victory.

paragraphs.structure: Six sentences. Opens with intimate, prophetic dream in accumulating horror, closes with waking cry. Dream sequence builds through repeated "and" conjunctions.
rhetorical-dna: Polysyndeton ("and... and... and") creating relentless dream-logic accumulation
Apocalyptic imagery (examples: bearded star, fiery signs, blood, flame)
Italicized prophetic utterance breaking narrative voice
Archaic vocabulary (adrad, lowe, betwixt) sustaining elevated register
paragraphs.respecification-seed: A prophetic nightmare foretells doom for a ruler's realm.
